title: The Mental Model Shift
description: Before you write a single line, you need to rewire how you think about software. AI isn't magic — it's software with learned parameters.
analogy: Think of pre-training like compiling source code into a binary

lessons.title: AI Is Just Software With Learned Parameters
Data Is the New Source Code
The AI Dev Lifecycle (It's Just CI/CD)
Set Up Your AI Playground
Your First Prompt: Calling an API in Natural Language

title: Neural Networks — The Computation Pipeline
description: Neural networks are just function composition pipelines where the functions have been optimized automatically. That's it.
analogy: An Express.js middleware chain where the handlers were learned from data

lessons.title: What Is a Neuron? (It's Just a Function)
Layers Are Pipeline Stages
Activation Functions: The Soft If/Else
The Forward Pass: Your Request Goes Through Middleware
Loss Functions: AI's Unit Test Assertions
Backpropagation: Reverse Git Blame
Gradient Descent: Binary Search for the Best Config
Learning Rate: Step Size Matters
Batches & Epochs: Pagination + Loops
Overfitting: Over-engineering Your Model
Regularization: Linting for Neural Nets

title: Embeddings — The Vector Database of Meaning
description: Embeddings give every word, sentence, or image a GPS coordinate in meaning-space. Similarity becomes distance.
analogy: A hash map where every key maps to a float array, and similar keys cluster nearby

lessons.title: What Are Embeddings? (Hash Maps to Vector Space)
Word2Vec & GloVe: Semantic Hash Codes
Vector Similarity: Fuzzy Matching at Scale
Embedding Dimensions: Schema Columns for Meaning
Sentence & Document Embeddings
Vector Databases: Semantic Search Indexes
Hands-On: Build a Semantic Search Engine

title: Tokenization — The Lexer of AI
description: Before a compiler can work, it needs a lexer. Before an LLM can work, it needs a tokenizer. Same idea.
analogy: String.split() on steroids — a custom lexer that learned from the whole internet

lessons.title: Tokenization: AI's Lexer
BPE: Text Compression as Tokenization
Token Vocabularies: Your Model's Enum
Context Windows: The Buffer Size of AI
Special Tokens: Reserved Keywords
Hands-On: Build a Tokenizer Visualizer

title: Transformers — The Game Changer
description: The transformer is the architecture that started it all. It's a data processing pipeline with a built-in search engine.
analogy: An event-driven pipeline where every token can query every other token simultaneously

lessons.title: Why Transformers Beat Everything Else
Self-Attention: A Database JOIN on Relevance
Query, Key, Value: The REST API of Attention
Multi-Head Attention: Parallel Search Queries
Positional Encoding: Array Indexes for AI
Encoder vs Decoder: Request Handler vs Response Builder
BERT vs GPT: Classifier vs Generator
Hands-On: Build Attention in 50 Lines

title: Large Language Models — The Revolution
description: LLMs are massive auto-complete engines trained on the internet. Understanding their scale, capabilities, and limits is essential for any engineer today.
analogy: The world's most sophisticated autocomplete — trained on a trillion-token codebase called 'the internet'

lessons.title: What Makes a Model 'Large'?
Next-Token Prediction: AI Is Just Autocomplete
Temperature & Sampling: Controlling Randomness
The GPT Family: Version Releases With Breaking Changes
Open Source LLMs: The Linux of AI
The Gemini Family: Google's Model Tiers
Emergent Abilities: Features That Appear at Scale

title: Prompt Engineering — Programming in Natural Language
description: The prompt is your API request body. Getting it right is the difference between a useless response and a perfectly structured output.
analogy: Writing function signatures and documentation — except the runtime is an LLM

lessons.title: Prompt Structure: System, User, Assistant (HTTP Headers)
Zero-Shot vs Few-Shot: No Examples vs Test Cases
Chain-of-Thought: console.log() Debugging for AI
Structured Output: Schema-Enforced API Responses
Prompt Templates: String Template Literals with Variables
Prompt Chaining: Function Composition for AI

title: Training & Fine-Tuning — Fork, Modify, Build
description: Pre-training gives you a foundation. Fine-tuning lets you customize it without rewriting everything.
analogy: Fork the repo, override the methods you care about — don't rewrite from scratch

lessons.title: Pre-training vs Fine-tuning: Source vs Library
RLHF: A/B Testing With Human Preference Data
LoRA: Monkey-Patching Your Model
QLoRA: LoRA But Memory-Efficient
Dataset Preparation: ETL for Training Data
Model Distillation: Minification for AI

title: Inference & Deployment — Shipping AI to Production
description: You've built the model. Now comes the hard part: shipping it reliably, efficiently, and at scale.
analogy: You've built the app — now containerize it, autoscale it, and monitor it

lessons.title: Quantization: Image Compression for Models
KV Cache: Connection Pooling + Memoization

Streaming Responses: SSE + WebSockets for AI

title: RAG & AI Applications — Building Real Products
description: Give your AI a search engine and a database. RAG is how you build AI that knows your data.
analogy: SELECT from your knowledge base, then ask the LLM to reason over the results

lessons.title: RAG: Give Your AI a Search Engine

title: Multimodal & Frontier AI — 2025–2026 Cutting Edge
description: The latest wave: AI that sees, hears, creates, and acts. What it means for your stack and career.
analogy: APIs that accept images, audio, and video — not just text strings
